title: Vincetoxicum tengii
taxonomy: Vincetoxicum tengii belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the genus Vincetoxicum. The genus Vincetoxicum is a group of plants that are native to Asia, and they are often found growing in woodland and grassland environments. Vincetoxicum tengii is a type of toxic plant, with its leaves and flowers containing toxic compounds that can be harmful if ingested. It is native to China, and its classification is as a perennial herbaceous plant.

intro: Vincetoxicum tengii, also known as Tengii, is a traditional Chinese medicinal plant belonging to the Apocynaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties, as well as its ability to promote cardiovascular health and immune function. The bioactive constituents of Vincetoxicum tengii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Vincetoxicum tengii is often prepared as a decoction or made into capsules, tablets, or injections for the treatment of various diseases, including cardiovascular and respiratory disorders.
